diagrams.title: Unix System Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the layered architecture of Unix from hardware to user applications
elements: Hardware (CPU, Memory, I/O Devices) at bottom
Kernel layer (system calls, process management, memory, file system, device drivers)
User Applications layer (editor, compiler, mail client)
Arrows showing system calls going upward from shell to kernel
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how user commands travel through Unix layers to access hardware

diagrams.title: Unix File System Hierarchy
diagrams.description: Standard Unix directory tree structure starting from root
elements: Subdirectories shown: /home contains /user1, /user2; /usr contains /bin, /lib, /local; /etc contains system config files
diagrams.purpose: Understand standard Unix directory organization and purpose of each directory

diagrams.title: Process States and Transitions
diagrams.description: State diagram showing Unix process lifecycle
elements: Boxes for states: Running, Ready, Sleeping, Stopped, Zombie
Arrows with labels: fork() creates, schedule() moves Running to Ready, I/O completion moves Sleeping to Ready, signal moves Running to Stopped, exit() creates Zombie, wait() reaps Zombie
PID labels at process creation points
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how processes move between states during their lifetime
